title: A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul

memes.description: 'Table of contents for A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul'

memes.label: "April"
memes.description: "Daily poems for April — 30 seven-line stanzas"

memes.label: "August"
memes.description: "Daily poems for August — 31 seven-line stanzas"

memes.label: Awe
memes.description: 'Awe in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — reverential wonder before the divine and the dead, sharply distinguished from mere [[fear]]; the "arctic awe" of encountering what lies beyond the ordinary, and the shame of finding only fear where awe should be.'

memes.label: "Beauty"
memes.description: 'Beauty in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — not mere aesthetic pleasure but a divine attribute breathed into creation by God, clothing the world in an "evening dress" that reveals rather than conceals the Creator''s nature.'

memes.label: Birth
memes.description: 'Birth and second birth in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — spiritual rebirth as the soul''s awakening to God, a slow process worked "by faint degrees" through centuries, culminating in the never-ending birth of the child of God.'

memes.label: "Bliss"
memes.description: 'Divine bliss in MacDonalds poetry — not a fleeting emotional state but the intrinsic quality of Gods own being, which overflows into the soul that loves God, transforming even the memory of earthly joy into its shadow.'

memes.label: "Body"
memes.description: 'In MacDonalds poetry, the body is not a prison to escape but a vessel of spiritual meaning — it grows poorer as the soul grows richer, and its very mortality serves the souls refinement toward God.'

memes.label: Bread
memes.description: 'Bread in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the symbol of spiritual sustenance received moment by moment from [[God]], not from "any human tilth"; bread must be received fresh, for "the holiest bread, if hoarded, soon will breed the mammon-moth" of spiritual [[pride]].'

memes.label: "Breath"
memes.description: 'In MacDonalds poetry, breath is the animating presence of God in the soul — as physical breath sustains the body, so Gods breath sustains spiritual life, and to be filled with divine breath is to become truly alive.'

memes.label: "Brother"
memes.description: "Spiritual brotherhood, Christ as elder brother, and the kinship of all souls in MacDonald's devotional vision"

memes.label: Calm
memes.description: 'Calm as spiritual state in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the inner stillness that God works in the soul through pain, the quieting of the heart''s swift throbs, and the calm resolve that trusts divine leading.'

memes.label: Child
memes.description: 'Spiritual childhood and the believer as child of God in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: Christ
memes.description: 'Christ as depicted in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry — the indwelling presence, the model of obedience, and the risen Lord who walks crowned'

memes.label: Clay
memes.description: 'Clay and potter imagery in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the human being as malleable material in the divine Potter''s hands, the wheel that runs slow, and the sparrow of clay that God makes to fly.'

memes.label: Conscience
memes.description: 'Conscience as the inner moral faculty through which God guides the soul, a key concept in MacDonald''s devotional ethics.'

memes.label: Conscious
memes.description: 'Consciousness in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the soul''s awareness of God and self, a faculty that is both a gift and a gulf; true consciousness is not self-reflection but God-illumined waking into eternal life.'

memes.label: "Creation"
memes.description: 'Creation in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the ongoing divine act by which God brings the soul and the world into being, and the theatre of the soul''s transformation.'

memes.label: Cross
memes.description: 'The cross in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the place of divine self-giving where Christ bids the speaker "hang with nails upon thy cross" (005-006); the pattern of suffering that shapes all [[discipleship]] as participation in the way of the crucified.'

memes.label: Cup
memes.description: 'The cup as spiritual vessel — of suffering, blessing, or divine outpouring — in MacDonald''s poetic imagery.'

memes.label: Darkness
memes.description: 'Spiritual darkness and its role in the soul''s growth in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: Death
memes.description: 'In MacDonald''s poetry, death is not an end but a transformation — a levelling that lifts, a willing self-surrender that opens into divine life'

memes.label: "December"
memes.description: "Daily poems for December — 31 seven-line stanzas"

memes.label: "Dedication"
memes.description: "Dedication and front matter of A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul"

memes.label: "Desire"
memes.description: 'Spiritual desire in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the God-given longing that draws the soul toward its divine source and ultimate satisfaction.'

memes.label: "Divine"
memes.description: "The divine nature in MacDonald's 'A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul' — God's essential character as love, light, and transforming power that indwells and reshapes the human soul."

memes.label: Door
memes.description: 'Door imagery in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the door as threshold between the human soul and God, a figure of both resistance and invitation, and the passage through death into divine life.'

memes.label: "Doubt"
memes.description: 'Spiritual doubt in MacDonalds poetry — not a sin to be suppressed but a "demon" to be faced honestly, the shadow-side of faith that, when brought to God, can become the very ground of deeper trust.'

memes.label: "Dream"
memes.description: "Dream as spiritual vision, divine communication, and the tension between illusion and transcendent truth in MacDonald's devotional poetry"

memes.label: "Duty"
memes.description: 'Duty and moral obligation in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — duty is transformed from external obligation into inward joy when the heart is aligned with God, such that obedience becomes delight rather than burden.'

memes.label: Earth
memes.description: 'Earth as the realm of human limitation and divine encounter in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: Emptiness
memes.description: 'Emptiness in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — not mere absence but the hollow that [[pride]] inflates and that [[God]] alone can press out; the "land of emptiness and dearth" given to some as a severe [[grace]] that drives the soul beyond the visible.'

memes.label: Eternal
memes.description: 'The eternal as the dimension of divine life breaking into time in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: Faint
memes.description: 'Spiritual faintness and weariness on the journey of faith, a recurring state in MacDonald''s devotional poetry.'

memes.label: Faith
memes.description: 'A central theme in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife" — the struggle for and gift of faith'

memes.label: "The Father"
memes.description: "The intimate, paternal face of God in George MacDonald's 'Diary of an Old Soul' — the Father whose 'plan' (006-001) governs the soul's transformation and whose loving parentage forms the relational core of MacDonald's theology."

memes.label: "Fear"
memes.description: "The duality of fear as holy reverence before God versus anxious dread, and its transformation through divine love in MacDonald's poetry"

memes.label: "February"
memes.description: "Daily poems for February — 29 seven-line stanzas"

memes.label: "Fire"
memes.description: "Fire in MacDonald's 'A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul' — the central metaphor for divine purification, the burning away of self that reveals the indestructible spirit-life within."

memes.label: "Freedom"
memes.description: 'Freedom in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife" — not autonomy but likeness to God, the soul''s slow fashioning into the divine image through constraint and surrender.'

memes.label: "Garment"
memes.description: 'The garment metaphor in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife" — divine covering as shelter and protection, and the spiritual necessity of being stripped naked before God.'

memes.label: George MacDonald
memes.description: 'George MacDonald (1824–1905), Scottish author, poet, and Christian minister — author of "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul"'

memes.label: Gift
memes.description: 'Gift in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the divine gift-nature of all that comes from God, the distinction between gift and Giver, and the soul''s dangerous tendency to clutch the gift while missing the hand that gives it.'

memes.label: "Glory"
memes.description: 'Divine glory in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — glory is the radiance of God''s perfect being that both reveals and conceals, overwhelming human sight while drawing the soul toward its source.'

memes.label: God
memes.description: 'The nature and attributes of God as addressed in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: "Grace"
memes.description: "Grace in MacDonald's 'A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul' — God's unmerited, active favor that not only forgives but transforms, refining the soul until no flaw remains that grace will tolerate."

memes.label: "Grief"
memes.description: 'Grief and mourning in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — grief is not a failure of faith but a condition God uses for the soul''s growth, a shadow that grace dispels and through which the heart is enlarged.'

memes.label: Growth
memes.description: 'Spiritual growth in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — not self-improvement but the organic unfolding of divine [[life]] within the soul; growth comes through [[trust]], aches toward fuller being, and may be swift or slow according to the Father''s wisdom.'

memes.label: Harvest
memes.description: 'Harvest in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the fruit of spiritual [[life]] whose withering reveals [[God]] in darkness, and whose golden yield depends not on natural cycle but on [[faith]] in the divine will alone.'

memes.label: "Heart"
memes.description: "In MacDonald's 'Diary of an Old Soul', the heart is the seat of spiritual receptivity — the organ through which divine love enters, transforms, and unites the human person with God."

memes.label: "Heaven"
memes.description: "Heaven in MacDonald's 'A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul' — not a distant afterlife destination but the immediate presence of God, entered through surrender of the will and already breaking into earthly life."

memes.label: Holy
memes.description: 'Holiness as divine attribute and human calling in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: "Home"
memes.description: 'In MacDonalds poetry, home is not a place but a person — God himself is the souls true dwelling, and the spiritual life is a journey homeward, drawn by the perfect love that awaits at the hearts origin.'

memes.label: "Hope"
memes.description: "Hope in MacDonald's 'A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul' — not optimistic wishing but confident trust in God's present goodness and future completion of the soul."

memes.label: "Human"
memes.description: 'Human nature as it stands before God in MacDonalds poetry — fallen, cloddish, and yet bearing the divine image, destined to be transformed by the indwelling Christ-self into something that reflects eternal light.'

memes.label: Humble
memes.description: 'Humility in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the soul''s proper posture before God, not self-abasement but the condition in which divine splendour is received; the "humble-bold" obedience that opens the window to God.'

memes.label: "Hunger"
memes.description: 'Spiritual hunger in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — not deprivation but the soul''s God-given appetite for righteousness and divine presence.'

memes.label: "Infinite"
memes.description: 'In MacDonalds poetry, infinity is the native element of the divine — Gods soul is the infinite ocean in which the human soul swims, and every approach to God draws the soul into an ever-deepening vastness that fills it with "infinite overflow."'

memes.label: "January"
memes.description: "Daily poems for January — 31 seven-line stanzas"

memes.label: "Joy"
memes.description: "Joy in MacDonald's 'A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul' — the spontaneous overflow of the soul's communion with God, not a pursuit but a byproduct of divine presence."

memes.label: "July"
memes.description: "Daily poems for July — 31 seven-line stanzas"

memes.label: "June"
memes.description: "Daily poems for June — 30 seven-line stanzas"

memes.label: Knocking
memes.description: 'The knocking in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the persistent divine approach figured as "knocking and knocking at the closed door" (001-007); the soul''s question whether "through the darkness come no knocking to our door" (011-003); the dialectic of closedness and opening that defines the soul''s relation to [[Christ]].'

memes.label: Lamb
memes.description: 'Lamb in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the speaker''s self-designation as a "stupid, self-willed, greedy lamb" who yet desires to become "honest and obedient"; the [[Christ]] who answers every prayer as the Lamb who was slain.'

memes.label: Law
memes.description: 'Divine law as the expression of God''s moral order, fulfilled through love and obedience in MacDonald''s devotional worldview.'

memes.label: Liberty
memes.description: 'Spiritual liberty as freedom from sin and self, found in surrender to God in MacDonald''s devotional worldview.'

memes.label: Life
memes.description: 'Spiritual life, eternal life, and Christ as life in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: Light
memes.description: 'Light as metaphor for divine illumination and Christ''s presence in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: "The Lord"
memes.description: "The primary divine addressee of George MacDonald's 'Diary of an Old Soul' — addressed over 90 times as 'Lord,' the personal God to whom the speaker pours out his struggles, petitions, and love."

memes.label: "Love"
memes.description: "The central theological concept in George MacDonald's 'A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul' — divine love as the transformative force that heals, purifies, and unites the soul with God."

memes.label: "March"
memes.description: "Daily poems for March — 31 seven-line stanzas"

memes.label: "Master"
memes.description: "God and Christ addressed as Master — not a tyrant but the skilled craftsman, teacher, and lord whose authority is love and whose work is the patient shaping of the soul."

memes.label: "May"
memes.description: "Daily poems for May — 31 seven-line stanzas"

memes.label: Might
memes.description: 'Divine might as God''s sustaining power, contrasted with human weakness in MacDonald''s devotional poetry.'

memes.label: "Mind"
memes.description: "The human mind as a channel for divine thought, and the eternal mind of God as the source of all understanding in MacDonald's poetry"

memes.label: "Morning"
memes.description: 'Morning in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife" as eschatological awakening — the moment when the soul finally sees God face to face and becomes fully itself.'

memes.label: "Mother"
memes.description: 'In MacDonalds poetry, motherhood is a divine metaphor — God is not only Father but carries mother-like tenderness, and the souls return to God is figured as a child running back to the mothers lap.'

memes.label: Need
memes.description: 'Spiritual need as the soul''s awareness of its dependence on God, the prerequisite for receiving divine grace in MacDonald''s poetry.'

memes.label: "Night"
memes.description: "Night as a metaphor for spiritual darkness, doubt, and the soul's restorative rest in MacDonald's devotional poetry"

memes.label: "November"
memes.description: "Daily poems for November — 30 seven-line stanzas"

memes.label: Obedience
memes.description: 'In MacDonald''s devotional poetry, obedience is the active proof of faith — doing God''s will even when feeling and understanding fail'

memes.label: "October"
memes.description: "Daily poems for October — 31 seven-line stanzas"

memes.label: "Pain"
memes.description: 'Pain in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — not meaningless torment but a divinely employed instrument of spiritual growth and a path toward God.'

memes.label: "Patience"
memes.description: 'In MacDonalds poetry, patience is not passive waiting but a forceful, active endurance — the souls capacity to "lie still" under Gods refining hand, trusting that divine timing is perfect and that present suffering is purposeful.'

memes.label: "Peace"
memes.description: 'Peace as spiritual rest in God in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — peace is not the absence of conflict but a gift strangely wrought from within strife, a fruit of surrender rather than escape.'

memes.label: "Perfect"
memes.description: "Divine perfection as the standard and destiny of love — God's perfect love calls forth the soul's perfecting, a gradual work accomplished by Christ in the believer."

memes.label: Poverty
memes.description: 'Spiritual poverty in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the soul''s honest recognition that it possesses nothing of its own, which "doth bow / Before the [[riches]] of thy making might" (003-002); the condition that makes exchange with divine wealth possible.'

memes.label: "Power"
memes.description: 'Divine power and human powerlessness in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — a radical theology of dependence in which God alone possesses creative power and the soul''s only true power is the will to receive.'

memes.label: "Prayer"
memes.description: 'Prayer in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the soul''s direct address to God, understood not as ritual request but as the living breath of relationship.'

memes.label: Presence
memes.description: 'The divine presence as source of life and object of longing in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: Pride
memes.description: 'Pride in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — "anxious pride" that doubts and distrusts [[God]]; "having-pride" that hoards spiritual [[gift]] until it breeds the "mammon-moth"; and "discovery''s pride" that mistakes the thrill of insight for the substance of [[faith]].'

memes.label: Rest
memes.description: 'Spiritual rest in the theology of George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — not mere cessation of labour but the soul''s peace in God, a state of trust so complete that action and rest become one; true rest is the opposite of the "rasp of unrest''s file" and can only be found in the divine presence.'

memes.label: Righteousness
memes.description: 'Righteousness in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — not self-achieved moral perfection but a hunger that [[God]] alone can satisfy; obedience lived in the day rather than claimed as possession.'

memes.label: "Rise"
memes.description: "The soul's upward movement toward God — rising as spiritual awakening, moral effort, and participation in Christ's resurrection, a central dynamic metaphor throughout MacDonald's poetry."

memes.label: "Seed"
memes.description: 'The seed metaphor in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife" — divine will implanted in the soul, growing through darkness and confinement toward an unknown flowering.'

memes.label: "Self"
memes.description: "The human self in its dual nature before God — the false, autonomous self that must be surrendered, and the true self that finds its being in union with the divine."

memes.label: "September"
memes.description: "Daily poems for September — 30 seven-line stanzas"

memes.label: Shadow
memes.description: 'Shadow as metaphor for partial knowledge and divine mystery in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: "Shame"
memes.description: 'Shame in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" as a redemptive instrument — divine chastening that drives the soul toward God rather than away.'

memes.label: Shepherd
memes.description: 'The Shepherd in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the divine figure who "leaves the ninety and nine" (009-014) to seek the straying [[lamb]]; the one into whose arms the speaker runs and is "folded" (009-016).'

memes.label: Silence
memes.description: 'Silence in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — not emptiness but the "Godlike" presence into which strife resolves; the "soul''s nest" where the noise of self gives way to divine [[love]] and [[trust]].'

memes.label: "Sin"
memes.description: "Sin in MacDonald's 'A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul' — not primarily legal transgression but separation from God through self-will and spiritual forgetfulness."

memes.label: "Sleep"
memes.description: "Sleep as both spiritual lethargy to be overcome and sacred rest in God's presence in MacDonald's devotional poetry"

memes.label: "Song"
memes.description: "Song as the soul's natural expression of divine praise, cosmic harmony, and the transformation of words into worship in MacDonald's poetry"

memes.label: "Sorrow"
memes.description: "Sorrow in MacDonald's 'A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul' — not merely painful emotion but a form of divine service, a purification of the heart that binds the soul closer to God and to others."

memes.label: Soul
memes.description: 'The soul, its journey toward God, and its transformation in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: "Spirit"
memes.description: "Spirit in MacDonald's 'A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul' — the divine presence indwelling the human soul, the agent of transformation, and the medium of the soul's communion with God."

memes.label: Strength
memes.description: 'Divine strength contrasted with human weakness; true strength comes from God alone in MacDonald''s poetry.'

memes.label: Strife
memes.description: 'Spiritual strife and conflict in the soul''s journey toward God in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: "Sun"
memes.description: "The sun as divine light, truth, and God's illuminating presence in MacDonald's devotional imagery"

memes.label: Tent
memes.description: 'The tent as image of the mortal body in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — a fragile, fluttering dwelling through which divine winds blow, a temporary shelter that the soul inhabits while journeying toward its permanent home in God.'

memes.label: "Thirst"
memes.description: 'Spiritual thirst in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the soul''s deep, God-given longing for divine presence, which is not a defect to be remedied but a sign of spiritual vitality and the very condition of its satisfaction.'

memes.label: Treasure
memes.description: 'Treasure in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the [[truth]] hidden in [[Christ]], the "treasure-field" worth every sacrifice, and the riches of the divine household that clothe the poor [[soul]]; always found where [[God]] is, never where the world stores its wealth.'

memes.label: "Trust"
memes.description: 'The act of entrusting oneself wholly to God — in MacDonalds view, trust is not passive resignation but actions highest kind, a living growth that deepens in the very presence of doubt.'

memes.label: "Truth"
memes.description: "Truth in MacDonald's 'A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul' — not abstract correctness but the person of God and the lived alignment of the soul with divine reality."

memes.label: Unrest
memes.description: 'Divine unrest in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the "deep unrest" (006-017) and "infinite unrest" (006-019) that God gives as a gift to drive the soul beyond its present contentment; the "rasp of unrest''s file" (008-002) that shapes the soul toward [[rest]].'

memes.label: Vessel
memes.description: 'Vessel in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the [[soul]] as ship captained not by the self but by [[God]], and as [[clay]] shaped by the divine potter; the vessel metaphor holds together human frailty and divine sovereignty in a single image of yielding.'

memes.label: Victory
memes.description: 'Victory in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — not the defeat of an enemy but the surrender of [[trust]]; the paradoxical triumph won by resting in [[faith]] and rising to "fight again."'

memes.label: Vision
memes.description: 'Spiritual vision in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the soul''s capacity to see God, the danger of vision voided through disobedience, and the beatific vision as the soul''s ultimate knowing.'

memes.label: "Wake"
memes.description: "Spiritual awakening from the sleep of sin and self into consciousness of divine presence in MacDonald's poetry"

memes.label: Walk
memes.description: 'Walking as metaphor for the daily spiritual life — the soul''s steady progress toward God in MacDonald''s poetry.'

memes.label: Water
memes.description: 'Water imagery in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — a metaphor for the divine life that sustains the soul, the troubled surface of imperfect perception, and the living water for which the [[soul]] thirsts on its pilgrimage through a dry land.'

memes.label: Way
memes.description: 'The spiritual path or "way" of following God, a recurring metaphor in MacDonald''s devotional poetry for the soul''s journey toward divine union.'

memes.label: Weakness
memes.description: 'Weakness in MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — human frailty not as defect but as the necessary condition for divine strength to manifest, the "weakness dire" through which God perfects the soul.'

memes.label: "Weary"
memes.description: "Spiritual weariness as a recurring state in the soul's journey — not sin but the honest exhaustion of the pilgrim who has walked long and still waits for the promised rest."

memes.label: Will
memes.description: 'The human will and its surrender to divine will in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: Wind
memes.description: 'Wind as image of the Holy Spirit and divine freedom in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: "Wisdom"
memes.description: 'Wisdom in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — not intellectual attainment or accumulated knowledge but a humble recognition of one''s own ignorance before God, whose love alone constitutes true understanding.'

memes.label: Wonder
memes.description: 'Spiritual wonder in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — the soul''s awed response to divine closeness, the astonishment that God should draw near to human badness, and the recognition that God''s ways exceed human comprehension; wonder is the native posture of the [[soul]] before its maker.'

memes.label: "Word"
memes.description: "The divine Word as creative power, the struggle of human words to convey truth, and the transformation of speech into song in MacDonald's devotional poetry"

memes.label: World
memes.description: 'The world as both divine creation and spiritual adversary in George MacDonald''s devotional poetry'

memes.label: "Worship"
memes.description: 'Worship in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — not ritual performance but whole-hearted orientation of the self toward God and neighbour, encompassing silent adoration at Christ''s feet and the worship of God through the recognition of the divine image in every fellow human being.'

memes.label: "Wrath"
memes.description: 'Wrath in George MacDonald''s "A Book of Strife in the Form of The Diary of an Old Soul" — distinguished between human wrath, which is spiteful and self-righteous, and divine wrath, which is a purified anger that serves love and consumes only what resists the good.'
